prose: Fukui reactivity indices for acrolein (CH₂=CH-CHO) were computed at the DFT level using tier 'standard' (B3LYP/def2-TZVP) in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Three single-point SCF calculations were run (neutral, N−1 cation doublet, N+1 anion doublet) and Fukui indices were derived from finite differences of Mulliken partial charges. Density fitting was OFF (exact four-center integrals). All three SCFs converged; integrity status = ok, trustworthy = true. No warnings were raised; charge conservation checks passed (sum(f⁺) = 1.0000, sum(f⁻) = 1.0000). The headline value reported is the largest f⁺ index: f⁺(C-4, β-carbon) = 0.64232102, identifying the β-carbon as the most electrophilic (preferred nucleophilic attack) site — consistent with Michael acceptor reactivity. The most nucleophilic site is O-1 (f⁻ = 0.39389). Per-atom results (8 atoms: O, C, C, C, H, H, H, H): f⁺ = [0.40136, 0.02226, 0.43405, 0.64232, −0.15726, −0.20720, −0.06212, −0.07341]; f⁻ = [0.39389, 0.00599, 0.09140, 0.08737, 0.08596, 0.18568, 0.08449, 0.06523]; dual descriptor = [+0.00748, +0.01627, +0.34264, +0.55495, −0.24322, −0.39288, −0.14660, −0.13863]. CLI: /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui acrolein.xyz --method dft --tier standard --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 1.
